name: Ace of Cups
interpretation: A clown gazes wistfully from the window of an elaborate cup-shaped sandcastle. The cup is ready to pour open into the world.
image_description: A massive cup made of sand and shells. Colorful shards decorate the six-sided base, and there are three sand dollars around the stem. The top of the cup resembles a sandcastle, with bursts of water pouring out of the bottom. It has six towers, each decorated with shells and topped with a spiral snail shell. There is a window in the center of the castle where a clown's white face is visible.

name: Two of Cups
interpretation: I titled this drawing "The Lady with the Little Dog," after Anton Chekhov's story of a poetic affair in Yalta. No words, just a chance glance at a seaside resort – the moment of shy magnetism.
image_description: Two Pierrots gaze at each other across a wrought iron fence by the seashore. The one in the background carries a white parasol and walks with a little white dog on a purple leash. Each Pierrot holds a yellow cup with a red interior.

name: Three of Cups
interpretation: Three clowns prepare their companion for her journey into the next world, keeping the essence of her with them.
image_description: Three Pierrot-morticians work on a fourth Pierrot who lies on a white-clothed table. One paints her face with a brush. Another reaches into a red cavity inside her shirt. A third carries off a jar of embalming fluid. There are three such jars: one contains a brain, another a heart, and the third a stomach.

name: Four of Cups
interpretation:
My vegetable love should grow
Vaster than empires and more slow

Andrew Marvell, "To His Coy Mistress"

Perhaps not noticing her would-be suitor, a clown pours all her affection into a plant. The appreciative plant slowly gains consciousness, but the suitor is out in the cold for now.
image_description:
Pierrot pours water from a teacup onto a large plant. Hands sprout from the top of the plant, one reaching down around the clown, and another holding a teacup. In the background, another Pierrot stands looking on, forlorn, with a bouquet of wilting stems.

The plant's pot matches the teapot and tea set next to it on the table: white china with a blue design featuring hearts and plant stems.

name: Five of Cups
interpretation: It's a natural jump from the very-French Pierrot to the Catholic nun clowns. For a little extracurricular activity, the nuns have decided to peek at what lies below their saint's robe. One cup is missing from the display – who is the thief?
image_description: Four nuns dressed in all white gather around a statue of a white clothed saint with a powdered face. The nun on the far left leans back and yawns, a red book resting on her chest. The nun next to her kneels with her hands together in prayer, looking toward the statue. To the right of her, another kneeling nun lifts up the saint's skirt, pointing at the red area underneath and looking back over her shoulder toward another giggling nun. The statue has a gold halo and a gold cross hanging on a string by its arms. There are three niches in the blue walls on either side of the statue. Five of the niches contain gold cups, the sixth is empty.

name: Six of Cups
interpretation: Like the clowns of the House of God, Pierrot is ready to take a fall, all for the sake of six lovely sirens. Can he swim? No matter, his ideal of love will keep him afloat.
image_description: Pierrot stands smiling, ready to dive, on the edge of a sailboat. In the flowing water around him there are six swimming women. The sailboat is yellow and its single sail matches Pierrot's baggy white lacy outfit. Pierrot also wears a black skullcap and green gloves and shoes.

name: Seven of Cups
interpretation: Following the scene of the Six of Cups, Pierrot plunges far below the surface, into the secret sacred depths of the ocean. He falls into one of the Star's rock gardens, a sanctuary of sea critters.
image_description: Pierrot floats underwater to a coral reef, surrounded by sea creatures. Behind him there is a large blue whale, so big that only one eye can be seen. Seven gold fish swim among bubbles around Pierrot. A green ray and a red octopus swim near the ocean floor by Pierrot's feet. On the floor in the middle of the reef, there is a spiral of pebbles. Seven gold cups are arranged along this spiral.

name: Eight of Cups
interpretation: Continuing from Seven of Cups, Pierrot moves through the submerged world to find his lovely Moon… or its reflection. He fills up cups, as souvenirs and gifts.
image_description: Climbing on a step ladder, Pierrot holds up a cup to catch water flowing from a crescent moon. The sky is pale blue with white circular stars, and the large moon is yellow. On the black ground there are seven more cups filled with water.

name: Nine of Cups
interpretation: Continuing from the Eight of Cups, the tide retreats and Pierrot finds himself washed up on shore. He will miss the ocean, but it is time to let go.
image_description: Pierrot is washed up on a sandy beach, his limbs spread out and his skullcap dislodged and covering one of his eyes. He surrounded by straggly pieces of red and green seaweed and nine clamshells, and the foamy tide rises up at edge of the card. His baggy white shirt has three blue buttons and one red button. He also wears blue gloves and shoes.

name: Ten of Cups
interpretation: Two white clowns put the finishing touch on their collaborative sandcastle, racing against the tide. Now that they've begun building, they're ready to establish their kingdom with the help of the suit of Coins.
image_description: One smiling Pierrot kneels down to work on a sandcastle, while another runs up to join in. He holds a tiny cup up in his hand triumphantly. This cup matches the nine cups already arranged in front of the sandcastle. The ocean tide is visible at the edge of the card. The Pierrot working on the castle has blue shoes and buttons, and green gloves. The other has green shoes and buttons, and blue gloves. The sandcastle is decorated with red, purple, and green shells.

name: Page of Cups
interpretation: The Page of Cups is ever hesitant, does he close his cup or leave it open?
image_description: Pierrot, looking puzzled, holds a large cup in his right hand and its lid in his left hand. An orange kitten at his feet looks up at the cup. He wears baggy white clothing with a black skullcap and black shoes with curly toes. His shirt has three buttons: red, green, and blue, and there is a six-petaled blue flower on his chest. The cup is gold with a red interior and a purple and red bauble decorated with hearts on its stem. The floor is pale blue, and a lacy white curtain hangs from the ceiling behind Pierrot.

name: Knight of Cups
interpretation: The Knight of Cups has a brighter outlook than the other court cards, ranging across the fields of sunflowers, guided by his heart.
image_description: Pierrot walks through a field of yellow flowers, followed by a calico cat. He smiles, holding up a cup in his right hand and pointing to it with his left. He wears baggy white clothing with lacy trim and pointed black shoes. He has a small pointed black hat decorated with a blue crescent moon over long yellow hair. The cup is yellow and gold, with a hexagonal top and a red interior. There is a purple and red bauble around the stem, and the three visible sides of the hexagon each have a red heart on them.

name: Queen of Cups
interpretation: The Queen of Cups sits peacefully entrenched in lace, a living doll.
image_description: Pierrot sits holding a lidded goblet in her right hand. She has a neutral expression. She wears an elaborately lacy white outfit with a cape and a cone-shaped hat over curly yellow hair. A white cat sleeps on a green cushion next to her chair. There are three buttons on her shirt, each decorated with a heart, in blue, red, and purple. She wears pale blue gloves and pointed red shoes, and there is a five-petaled blue flower on the tip of her hat. The cup is yellow with red decorations. The floor is tiled in black and white checkers.

name: King of Cups
interpretation: The King of Cups has the classic Pierrot posture of woe, but he loves his melancholy.
image_description: Pierrot, sitting on a stool, raises his left hand to his forehead with a look of anguish. He holds a goblet in his right hand and a black and white cat climbs on his shoulders. He wears baggy white clothing and a white cone-shaped hat. His shoes are red and pointed, there are three blue buttons on his shirt, and four yellow buttons on his hat. The goblet is yellow with a red interior and a red band around it. The floor is tiled in black and white checkers and an ivory-colored curtain hangs behind him, partly covering a potted plant with pointed leaves.
